components.name: Accessory Protocol Interface Module
components.description: Synthetic infotainment and connectivity module representing Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, USB, mobile-device integration, applications, media, navigation and user-facing services.
components.role: Externally exposed infotainment domain and potential pivot point toward in-vehicle networks.
components.external_interfaces: Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, USB, mobile devices, application services, Automotive Ethernet to GWM
components.security_focus: Application isolation, software integrity, privacy, interface hardening, update authenticity, logging, lateral-movement prevention

components.name: Gateway Module
components.description: Synthetic central gateway responsible for routing, trust-boundary enforcement, diagnostic-session control and separation of exposed and protected vehicle domains.
components.role: Primary policy-enforcement point between APIM/TCU and protected vehicle networks.
components.external_interfaces: Automotive Ethernet, DoIP, CAN, CAN-FD, UDS diagnostic services
components.security_focus: Segmentation, signed routing policy, UDS authorization, message allowlisting, anomaly detection, evidence-quality logging

components.name: Telematics Control Unit
components.description: Synthetic externally connected vehicle module representing cellular connectivity, cloud communication, vehicle identity, telemetry, remote commands and OTA staging.
components.role: Remote vehicle entry point and trusted intermediary between cloud services and the GWM.
components.external_interfaces: Cellular, GNSS, OEM cloud, OTA backend, Automotive Ethernet/DoIP to GWM
components.security_focus: Remote attack surface, mTLS, certificate lifecycle, secure boot, OTA integrity, cloud authorization, fleet monitoring

components.name: OEM Cloud Context
components.description: Contextual external system for identity, remote services, telemetry and OTA orchestration. Not assessed as a complete cloud platform in this prototype.
components.external_interfaces: Internet APIs, mutual TLS, campaign and telemetry services

components.name: Protected Vehicle Networks
components.description: Contextual destination representing safety-relevant, body, powertrain and other domains behind the GWM.
components.external_interfaces: CAN, CAN-FD, Ethernet and diagnostic routes through GWM

interfaces.description: Remote commands, telemetry, software-update metadata and package transfer.

interfaces.trust_boundary: Externally connected ECU to central gateway
interfaces.description: Authorized diagnostic and update traffic from TCU toward vehicle networks.
Infotainment services and controlled access to vehicle data.

assets.name: TCU software integrity
assets.description: The TCU must execute only authorized software and preserve trusted behavior at the remote vehicle boundary.

assets.name: Vehicle identity and private keys
assets.description: Certificates and private keys used to authenticate the vehicle to cloud services.

assets.name: Gateway routing policy
assets.description: Signed configuration that determines permitted traffic between domains.

assets.name: Diagnostic authorization state
assets.description: Session and authorization state controlling UDS access and programming services.

assets.name: APIM software and application boundary
assets.description: Infotainment operating environment and application separation.

assets.name: Customer profile and token data
assets.description: User profiles, tokens and privacy-relevant infotainment data.

assets.name: Cross-module security event traceability
assets.description: Ability to correlate APIM, TCU and GWM events into one defensible attack narrative.

threats.title: Remote TCU compromise followed by diagnostic pivot
threats.mitre_techniques: T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application; T1548 Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism (analogous mapping)
threats.scenario: A remote attacker exploits an exposed TCU service, gains execution, initiates a DoIP session toward the GWM and attempts SecurityAccess and RequestDownload against a protected route.
threats.vulnerability: Memory-safety weakness or exposed service combined with insufficient process isolation and incomplete fleet-event correlation.

threats.title: Compromised APIM attempts cross-domain message injection
threats.stride_categories: Tampering; Elevation of privilege; Information disclosure
threats.mitre_techniques: T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application; T1552 Unsecured Credentials (where applicable)
threats.scenario: An attacker compromises an APIM application or interface and attempts to send a message toward a protected domain through the GWM.
threats.vulnerability: Externally exposed media interfaces, vulnerable third-party application or insecure token storage.

threats.title: Gateway policy tampering or rollback
threats.mitre_techniques: T1562.001 Impair Defenses; T1070 Indicator Removal (analogous mapping)
threats.scenario: An attacker modifies or replays an older routing policy so previously prohibited APIM or TCU traffic is allowed toward protected networks.
threats.vulnerability: Unsigned policy, weak rollback protection or insufficiently protected policy-update process.

threats.title: Revoked certificate remains usable during connectivity outage
threats.scenario: A revoked TCU identity remains accepted because cached sessions or stale revocation information are used while the vehicle is offline.
threats.vulnerability: Revocation freshness and cached-session policy do not fully address extended connectivity loss.

threats.title: Compromised supplier APIM package passes ordinary signing checks
threats.scenario: Malicious or compromised code is introduced before OEM signing and is then distributed as an apparently legitimate APIM package.
threats.vulnerability: Incomplete source-to-binary provenance, partial composition analysis and overreliance on package signatures.

tara_records.damage_scenario: Unauthorized diagnostic programming reaches a protected vehicle domain, potentially altering ECU software or configuration and impairing vehicle operation.
tara_records.attack_path: Remote cellular entry → exploit TCU service → elevate privileges → initiate DoIP toward GWM → request UDS SecurityAccess → attempt RequestDownload toward protected route.
tara_records.cybersecurity_goal: Only authenticated, authorized and policy-approved entities may initiate diagnostic programming through the GWM.

tara_records.damage_scenario: A compromised infotainment environment injects unauthorized traffic toward protected networks or exposes customer information.
tara_records.attack_path: Bluetooth/Wi-Fi/third-party application compromise → APIM execution → attempt prohibited message through GWM → protected-network target.
tara_records.cybersecurity_goal: APIM-originated traffic shall be restricted to explicitly authorized messages and routes.

tara_records.damage_scenario: Modified gateway policy removes network separation, allowing untrusted domains to reach protected systems.
tara_records.attack_path: Compromise policy-update authority or storage → alter or replay policy → GWM loads unauthorized policy → prohibited traffic is routed.
tara_records.cybersecurity_goal: The GWM shall load only authentic, current and authorized routing policy and shall fail securely if verification is unavailable.

tara_records.damage_scenario: A revoked or stolen vehicle identity remains usable, enabling unauthorized cloud access or impersonation.
tara_records.attack_path: Obtain revoked credential/session → exploit stale revocation state during outage → authenticate as legitimate TCU → issue or receive unauthorized service traffic.
tara_records.cybersecurity_goal: Revoked vehicle identities shall become unusable within a defined maximum interval, including during degraded connectivity.
tara_records.analyst_notes: Demonstrates nuanced evidence assessment rather than binary compliance.

tara_records.damage_scenario: A supplier-originated malicious component executes within APIM and creates privacy, availability or lateral-movement exposure.
tara_records.attack_path: Supplier development compromise → malicious code in binary → normal OEM signing → APIM update installation → malicious behavior activates.
tara_records.cybersecurity_goal: Supplier software shall have traceable provenance and sufficient independent assurance before approval for APIM release.

controls.title: Diagnostic service authorization
controls.objective: Block unauthorized diagnostic-session escalation and programming services from APIM, TCU or other unapproved sources.
controls.implementation: Source-aware DoIP policy, session-state validation, UDS SecurityAccess enforcement and explicit deny rules for programming services.
controls.verification: Attempt 0x27 and 0x34 from unauthorized TCU/APIM routes and confirm NRC response, non-forwarding, local log and fleet alert.

controls.title: Signed routing policy and anti-rollback
controls.objective: Ensure gateway routing policy is authentic, current and fail-secure.
controls.implementation: Signature verification, monotonic policy version, protected key service and restricted-routing fallback.
controls.verification: Modify policy, replay older signed policy and remove key service during boot.

controls.title: Cross-domain message allowlist
controls.objective: Permit only explicitly authorized source, destination and message combinations.
controls.implementation: Message-ID allowlist, source-zone binding and alert on denied cross-domain traffic.
controls.verification: Inject prohibited APIM-originated message and confirm drop plus alert.

controls.title: Secure boot and signed software
controls.objective: Prevent unauthorized TCU software from executing.
controls.implementation: Hardware-rooted secure boot, signed update packages and measured boot records.
controls.verification: Attempt modified, unsigned and rolled-back TCU software.

controls.title: Vehicle identity and certificate lifecycle
controls.objective: Authenticate TCU-cloud communications and make revoked identities unusable within a defined interval.
controls.implementation: Mutual TLS, protected private keys, certificate validation, revocation checks and bounded cache lifetime.
controls.verification: Test valid, expired, untrusted and revoked certificates in online and offline states.

controls.title: Application isolation and interface hardening
controls.objective: Limit compromise through media and application interfaces and contain compromised applications.
controls.implementation: Sandboxing, least privilege, parser hardening, token protection and restricted vehicle-service APIs.
controls.verification: Fuzz interfaces, inspect token storage and attempt lateral movement through GWM.

controls.title: Supplier software provenance and composition assurance
controls.objective: Establish sufficient confidence that APIM supplier binaries correspond to reviewed and approved content.
controls.implementation: SBOM, secure-development attestation, independent binary composition analysis, vulnerability scan and source-to-binary provenance evidence.
controls.verification: Reject release artifact when required supplier evidence is absent or inconsistent.

controls.title: Cross-module event correlation
controls.objective: Link TCU, APIM and GWM events into one traceable security incident.
controls.implementation: Shared vehicle event identifier, synchronized time, immutable event metadata and fleet correlation rules.
controls.verification: Replay TCU-to-GWM attack and confirm one correlated high-confidence incident is generated.

frameworks.name: ISO/SAE 21434
frameworks.notes: Prototype stores user-authored summaries and references, not standard text.

frameworks.name: UN Regulation No. 155
frameworks.notes: Mappings are illustrative and require regulatory review.

frameworks.name: UN Regulation No. 156
frameworks.notes: Used for update governance, integrity and traceability.

frameworks.name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
frameworks.notes: Mapped at function/category level for prototype clarity.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27001
frameworks.notes: Annex A references require verification against a licensed copy.

frameworks.name: Internal OEM Cybersecurity Policy
frameworks.notes: No real Ford policy or proprietary process is included.

frameworks.name: MITRE ATT&CK
frameworks.notes: Analogous mappings only; ATT&CK is not an automotive compliance standard.

requirements.title: Structured threat analysis and risk assessment
requirements.summary: Identify assets, damage scenarios, threat scenarios, attack feasibility, impact and risk treatment using an approved method.
requirements.applicability: All APIM, GWM and TCU TARA records
requirements.verification_note: Review complete traceability and rationale; verify organization-approved scoring method in production.

requirements.title: Cybersecurity goals derived from risk
requirements.summary: Derive cybersecurity goals and claims from assessed risks and item definition.
requirements.verification_note: Trace each goal to one or more TARA records.

requirements.title: Requirements, verification and evidence traceability
requirements.summary: Maintain traceability from cybersecurity goals to requirements, implementation and verification evidence.
requirements.verification_note: Confirm implementation and test evidence are linked and reviewed.

requirements.title: Identify, assess and mitigate vehicle cyber risks
requirements.summary: Demonstrate a managed process for identifying relevant risks and implementing mitigations throughout the vehicle lifecycle.
requirements.verification_note: Review risk records, treatment decisions, ownership and reassessment.

requirements.title: Monitor and respond to attacks and vulnerabilities
requirements.summary: Support detection, analysis and response for cybersecurity events and emerging vulnerabilities.
requirements.verification_note: Verify events are logged, correlated, escalated and linked to response actions.

requirements.title: Manage supplier cybersecurity dependencies
requirements.summary: Obtain and evaluate sufficient supplier cybersecurity assurance for relevant components and software.
requirements.verification_note: Review SBOM, vulnerability analysis, provenance, testing and remediation obligations.

requirements.title: Protect software-update authenticity and integrity
requirements.summary: Ensure software updates are authorized, traceable and protected against unauthorized modification or rollback.
requirements.applicability: TCU and APIM updates; GWM policy updates
requirements.verification_note: Review signature, version, authorization, rollback and record evidence.

requirements.title: Apply safeguards to manage cybersecurity risk
requirements.summary: Use identity, access, platform security and data-security safeguards appropriate to the assessed risk.
requirements.verification_note: Map control implementation and tests to the relevant outcome.

requirements.title: Detect and analyze anomalous activity
requirements.summary: Monitor assets and services and analyze potentially adverse events.
requirements.verification_note: Confirm event quality, correlation and escalation behavior.

requirements.title: Logging and monitoring activities
requirements.summary: Produce, protect, review and monitor logs supporting detection and investigation.
requirements.verification_note: Verify completeness, integrity, access control, retention and monitoring.

requirements.title: Appropriate use of cryptography
requirements.summary: Define and apply cryptographic controls and key-management practices appropriate to risk.
requirements.applicability: TCU identity, signed GWM policy and software updates
requirements.verification_note: Review algorithms, key protection, lifecycle and validation tests.

requirements.title: Supplier relationship security
requirements.summary: Define, monitor and manage information-security obligations in supplier relationships.
requirements.verification_note: Review contractual requirements, evidence and ongoing monitoring.

requirements.title: Gateway diagnostic isolation
requirements.summary: The GWM shall reject unauthorized programming requests from externally exposed vehicle domains and report the attempt to fleet monitoring.
requirements.applicability: GWM diagnostic routes from TCU and APIM
requirements.verification_note: Require prevention, local logging and centralized alert evidence.

requirements.title: Cross-module event correlation
requirements.summary: Security events forming one attack path shall share sufficient identifiers for automated correlation and escalation.
requirements.verification_note: Replay attack and confirm a single correlated incident.

requirements.title: Supplier software transparency
requirements.summary: APIM supplier releases shall include an SBOM, vulnerability analysis, secure-development evidence and agreed provenance artifacts.
requirements.verification_note: Reject or escalate release when mandatory evidence is absent.

requirements.title: Systematic threat-category review
requirements.summary: Review each relevant trust boundary for spoofing, tampering, repudiation, information disclosure, denial of service and elevation of privilege.
requirements.verification_note: Confirm category review is documented, not treated as a final risk rating.

requirements.title: Adversary behavior enrichment
requirements.summary: Associate applicable ATT&CK techniques with threat scenarios to enrich detection and response analysis.
requirements.applicability: Remote and supply-chain scenarios where mapping is defensible
requirements.verification_note: Require analyst confirmation and document that mapping is analogous where not vehicle-specific.

control_mappings.rationale: The control converts a risk-derived goal into testable gateway behavior and evidence.
It treats the diagnostic-pivot risk identified in TARA-TCU-001.
The synthetic OEM requirement directly calls for denial and reporting.
Diagnostic authorization is an access-control and platform-protection safeguard.
Gateway policy is software/configuration requiring authenticity, authorization and rollback protection.
Policy-signature and key-management controls apply cryptography to configuration integrity.
Network segmentation limits lateral movement from exposed domains.
Secure boot and signed software protect update authenticity and integrity.
Vehicle identity depends on cryptography and certificate lifecycle management.
The control addresses tampering, information disclosure and elevation-of-privilege threats identified at APIM interfaces.
Supplier assurance evidence supports supply-chain risk management.
The control supports supplier relationship security and monitoring.
It implements the synthetic supplier evidence requirement.
Correlation improves monitoring, detection and response.
It enables detection and analysis of related adverse events.
It improves logging and monitoring effectiveness.
It directly implements the synthetic cross-module correlation requirement.

evidence.title: Remote exploit indicators
evidence.summary: Shows malformed external input, process exception, unexpected outbound connection, privilege transition and unscheduled DoIP discovery.

evidence.title: UDS enforcement log
evidence.summary: GWM denied SecurityAccess and RequestDownload and did not forward the request; centralized alert was missing.

evidence.title: Policy integrity and rollback test
evidence.summary: Modified and replayed policy were rejected; fail-secure behavior observed with a recovery observation.

evidence.title: APIM interface penetration test
evidence.summary: Shows token-storage weakness and attempted APIM-to-protected-network movement blocked by GWM.

evidence.title: Certificate revocation test
evidence.summary: Revoked certificates were rejected online but cached session behavior remained permissive during simulated outage.

evidence.title: Supplier SBOM and provenance review
evidence.summary: SBOM and attestation exist, but source-to-binary provenance and independent composition analysis are incomplete.

evidence.title: Cross-module correlation gap
evidence.summary: TCU and GWM events were logged separately without a shared identifier and required manual correlation.

evidence.title: TCU 5.3 certificate regression plan
evidence.summary: Defines required 5.3 certificate and degraded-connectivity regression cases; execution is pending.

evidence.title: TCU 5.3 DoIP/UDS regression
evidence.summary: Prevention remains effective in partial testing, but repeatability, state coverage, alerting, and correlation remain incomplete.

evidence.title: TCU 5.3 SBOM difference
evidence.summary: Shows dependency changes and the high-severity CertCore vulnerability affecting the candidate release.

evidence.title: Correlation rule validation
evidence.summary: TCU emits a shared ID but GWM/fleet mapping does not consume it; one correlated incident is not produced.

assessments.rationale: The GWM prevented unauthorized SecurityAccess and RequestDownload and did not forward the request. The synthetic OEM requirement also requires fleet reporting, but no centralized alert was generated.
assessments.recommendation: Preserve the denial behavior and add a high-severity fleet alert containing source zone, session identifier, requested service, target route and vehicle event correlation ID.

assessments.rationale: TCU and GWM evidence describe the same attack path but lack a shared event identifier. The fleet monitor created separate low-confidence events requiring manual correlation.
assessments.recommendation: Generate a common vehicle security event ID at the first boundary crossing and propagate it through TCU, DoIP, GWM policy and fleet-monitoring records.

findings.title: TCU and GWM events cannot be automatically correlated
findings.recommendation: Implement shared event identifiers and a fleet correlation rule for remote-entry-to-diagnostic-pivot sequences.

campaigns.title: 2026 APIM–GWM–TCU Cybersecurity Assurance Review
campaigns.objective: Assess whether risk-derived controls are appropriately designed, implemented and operating effectively for the synthetic APIM, GWM and TCU baseline.
campaigns.scope: APIM, GWM, TCU, relevant cloud, diagnostic and in-vehicle interfaces

campaign_components.notes: Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, USB, application and update paths.
DoIP, UDS authorization, segmentation, routing and event reporting.
Cellular, cloud identity, OTA staging, telemetry and gateway access.

applicability.rationale: The TCU and APIM are exposed domains that can initiate DoIP traffic toward the GWM; gateway diagnostic isolation is directly relevant.
The remote TCU attack surface creates risk scenarios that require structured threat and risk analysis.
APIM software is supplier-developed and requires assurance artifacts before release.
The TCU stages and authenticates remote software updates.
Governance outcomes apply at program level rather than as an APIM firmware behavior.

evidence_requests.requested_evidence: Approved gateway policy; software baseline; UDS capture for unauthorized 0x27 and 0x34; local GWM event; fleet-monitoring alert; test procedure and result.
evidence_requests.sufficiency_notes: Prevention and local logging are evidenced; centralized alerting and shared event correlation are not demonstrated.

evidence_requests.requested_evidence: Certificate policy; online and offline revocation test; package-signature test; software version; OTA sequence; failure logs.
evidence_requests.sufficiency_notes: Offline cache behavior requires assessment and a documented maximum freshness period.

evidence_requests.requested_evidence: Supplier SBOM; signed package; vulnerability scan; secure-development attestation; source-to-binary provenance; independent composition analysis.
evidence_requests.sufficiency_notes: SBOM and supplier attestations exist; reproducibility and independent composition evidence remain incomplete.

control_evaluations.rationale: The control design addresses source-aware diagnostic authorization and the GWM demonstrably denied unauthorized 0x27 and 0x34 requests. The operating evidence does not demonstrate centralized alerting or automatic TCU-to-GWM event correlation.
control_evaluations.recommendation: Retain deny behavior; add fleet alerting, a shared event identifier, and end-to-end retest evidence.

control_evaluations.rationale: Online certificate validation and revocation are demonstrated. A cached session remains accepted during a prolonged connectivity outage, leaving revocation-freshness behavior insufficiently bounded.
control_evaluations.recommendation: Define maximum offline cache lifetime and fail behavior; repeat outage and reconnection tests.

corrective_actions.root_cause: GWM and TCU logging requirements were developed independently without an end-to-end vehicle-security correlation requirement.
corrective_actions.action_plan: Add a shared vehicle-security event identifier, emit a high-severity fleet alert, and implement a correlation rule for the TCU compromise to GWM diagnostic-pivot sequence.
corrective_actions.interim_mitigation: Daily analyst review of TCU DoIP anomalies and GWM diagnostic-denial events using time and synthetic VIN.
corrective_actions.retest_notes: Retest must verify one correlated fleet incident and preservation of existing deny behavior.

supplier_assessments.recommendation: Refresh SBOM for each release candidate.

supplier_assessments.gap: No reproducible-build or source-to-binary traceability evidence.
supplier_assessments.recommendation: Require signed build provenance and independent composition analysis before release approval.

supplier_assessments.gap: Offline revocation-freshness behavior is not bounded.
supplier_assessments.recommendation: Define cache lifetime and resubmit outage/reconnection test.

profile_outcomes.title: Access permissions and authorizations are managed
profile_outcomes.current_state: Unauthorized diagnostic programming is denied locally.
profile_outcomes.target_state: Unauthorized attempts are denied, logged, centrally reported and correlated.
profile_outcomes.action: Implement fleet alerting and shared event identifiers.

profile_outcomes.title: Networks and services are monitored for adverse events
profile_outcomes.current_state: Separate TCU and GWM events exist with low-confidence manual correlation.
profile_outcomes.target_state: One high-confidence correlated incident is generated automatically.
profile_outcomes.action: Create correlation rule and common telemetry schema.

profile_outcomes.title: Cybersecurity supply-chain risk is managed
profile_outcomes.current_state: SBOM and supplier attestation are available; provenance is incomplete.
profile_outcomes.target_state: Complete SBOM, signed provenance and independent composition analysis for every release.
profile_outcomes.action: Update supplier evidence requirements and release gate.

profile_outcomes.title: Update authenticity and certificate status remain trustworthy
profile_outcomes.current_state: Online validation works; cached sessions can outlive current revocation status.
profile_outcomes.target_state: Offline cache lifetime and fail behavior are formally bounded and tested.
profile_outcomes.action: Define policy and retest extended outage behavior.

audit_questions.question: What exactly is inside and outside this assessment campaign, and which software versions were assessed?
audit_questions.expected_points: Name APIM 8.4.2, GWM 6.4.0, TCU 5.2.1; describe production-equivalent bench; state that protected ECUs and real OEM processes are context, not assessed implementation.

audit_questions.question: Why is the GWM diagnostic-isolation requirement applicable to this campaign?
audit_questions.expected_points: TCU and APIM are exposed domains; both can reach the GWM; unauthorized DoIP/UDS movement could affect protected routes; applicability ties architecture and TARA risk to the requirement.

audit_questions.question: Why is one denied RequestDownload log not enough to declare the requirement fully met?
audit_questions.expected_points: The full requirement includes prevention, local logging, fleet reporting, scope/version evidence and repeatable testing. One event is relevant but incomplete and may not prove operating effectiveness.

audit_questions.question: Explain the difference between design effectiveness, implementation and operating effectiveness for CTRL-GWM-001.
audit_questions.expected_points: Design: would source-aware policy satisfy the requirement; implementation: policy exists in GWM 6.4.0; operating effectiveness: representative tests show it consistently denies, logs and reports.

audit_questions.question: Who can accept the remaining risk, and what evidence would you require before closure?
audit_questions.expected_points: Authorized risk owner/approver, not AI or assessor alone; corrective action completion evidence; end-to-end retest; centralized alert; correlation; no regression in deny behavior.

audit_questions.question: Does mapping CTRL-GWM-001 to several sources prove compliance with all of them?
audit_questions.expected_points: No. A mapping shows a relationship, not conformance. Each source has scope, process and evidence expectations. Organizational obligations cannot be proven solely by one technical test.

software_releases.notes: Candidate release changes certificate validation, DoIP retry behavior, and telemetry event schema.
Current gateway baseline interacting with both TCU releases.
Current APIM baseline used for supplier-assurance examples.

release_changes.title: Certificate validation library update
release_changes.description: CertCore changes from 2.8.4 to 3.1.0, altering certificate-chain, revocation-cache, and failure-handling behavior.
release_changes.affected_interfaces: Cellular mTLS; OEM cloud identity service
release_changes.cybersecurity_impact: Prior revocation and degraded-connectivity test evidence cannot be reused without regression testing.

release_changes.title: DoIP retry and route-selection change
release_changes.description: The TCU now retries DoIP discovery after a route denial and adds a fallback target selection path.
release_changes.cybersecurity_impact: GWM isolation must be retested with the new source behavior even though GWM software is unchanged.

release_changes.title: Vehicle security event identifier added
release_changes.description: TCU telemetry adds a security_event_id field, but the GWM-to-fleet mapping has not been updated to consume the field.
release_changes.cybersecurity_impact: The change creates an opportunity to close the correlation gap, but end-to-end validation remains required.

evidence_lifecycle.stale_reason: TCU process and DoIP behavior changed.
Interacting TCU source behavior changed; previous GWM result does not cover retries and fallback routing.
New TCU event field is not yet mapped through the GWM/fleet pipeline.
New certificate regression evidence requested but not approved.
End-to-end DoIP/UDS regression test not yet approved.
SBOM is current but identifies an unresolved high-severity library vulnerability.
Correlation rule validation has not been completed.

vulnerabilities.reachability: Conditional — reachable during mTLS certificate parsing
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to CertCore 3.1.1 or apply vendor mitigation; repeat online/offline revocation and malformed-chain tests.

vulnerabilities.exploitability: Mitigated by input bounds and service isolation

change_impacts.impact_reason: Certificate validation and cache behavior changed.
change_impacts.action: Reassess feasibility and repeat certificate lifecycle tests.

change_impacts.impact_reason: New DoIP retries and fallback selection exercise gateway policy differently.
Prior test did not cover the new TCU retry behavior.
New event identifier can improve correlation but the downstream mapping is incomplete.
change_impacts.action: Update rule mapping and validate one correlated incident.

change_impacts.target_title: Attack attempts are detected and correlated
change_impacts.impact_reason: Claim remains only partially supported until end-to-end correlation is proven.

detection_rules.title: Unexpected DoIP programming sequence
detection_rules.behavior: Externally exposed TCU initiates unscheduled DoIP followed by unauthorized UDS 0x27 or 0x34.
detection_rules.telemetry_sources: TCU process events; DoIP connections; GWM source zone; UDS service; NRC; target route; fleet event ID
detection_rules.logic_summary: Correlate TCU privilege transition + unexpected DoIP + GWM denial within 120 seconds using security_event_id.
detection_rules.response_playbook: Block route; preserve logs; open fleet incident; identify software baseline; begin vulnerability triage.

detection_rules.title: Revoked or anomalous vehicle identity use
detection_rules.behavior: Revoked, expired, or malformed certificate is presented or a cached identity remains active beyond policy.
detection_rules.telemetry_sources: mTLS result; revocation freshness; certificate fingerprint; cache age; connectivity state
detection_rules.logic_summary: Alert on revoked identity acceptance, cache age beyond policy, or repeated malformed-chain failures.
detection_rules.response_playbook: Terminate session; revoke cache; quarantine identity; investigate fleet exposure; request supplier analysis.

cybersecurity_claims.title: APIM–GWM–TCU platform is acceptably secure for assessed use
cybersecurity_claims.statement: The scoped platform manages remote, infotainment, diagnostic, update, identity, and monitoring risks to an acceptable residual level for the approved baseline.
cybersecurity_claims.rationale: Prevention controls are demonstrated, but release 5.3 has unresolved evidence, vulnerability, and correlation gaps.

cybersecurity_claims.title: Unauthorized diagnostic programming is prevented
cybersecurity_claims.statement: TCU or APIM compromise does not permit unauthorized UDS programming through the GWM.
cybersecurity_claims.rationale: GWM 6.4 blocked prior behavior, but TCU 5.3 changes retries and route selection.

cybersecurity_claims.title: Vehicle identity lifecycle is enforced
cybersecurity_claims.statement: Invalid, expired, revoked, or malformed TCU identities are rejected within policy-defined limits.
cybersecurity_claims.rationale: New CertCore version has an open vulnerability and regression evidence is pending.

cybersecurity_claims.title: Attack attempts are detected and correlated
cybersecurity_claims.statement: TCU and GWM events forming one attack path generate one actionable fleet incident.
cybersecurity_claims.rationale: Local events exist, but shared-ID mapping and end-to-end correlation are not validated.

claim_evidence.notes: Demonstrates denial for TCU 5.2 behavior only.
Shows manual rather than automatic correlation.

release_gates.blockers: Open high vulnerability CVE-SYNTH-2026-0421; mandatory evidence EVD-TCU-003/EVD-TCU-004/EVD-DET-001 pending; diagnostic and monitoring claims not fully supported.
release_gates.conditions: Resolve or formally mitigate vulnerability; approve certificate regression; approve DoIP/UDS regression; validate one correlated fleet incident.
release_gates.notes: AI may calculate posture but cannot approve release.
